Title: User Registration
Test Data:
Username: testuser
Password: testpassword
Email: testuser@example.com
Test Steps:
1. Open the application registration page.
2. Enter the username, password, and email address.
3. Click on the "Register" button.
4. Verify that the registration process completes successfully.
Expected Result: The user should be registered successfully, and a confirmation message should appear.

Title: User Login
Test Steps:
1. Open the application login page.
2. Enter the username and password.
3. Click on the "Login" button.
4. Verify that the user is logged in successfully.
Expected Result: The user should be logged in successfully, and a welcome message should appear.

Title: Add a Report
Test Data:
Incident Type: Theft
Location: Main Street
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the report submission page.
2. Select the incident type as "Theft".
3. Enter the location as "Main Street".
4. Click on the "Submit" button.
5. Verify that the report is submitted successfully.
Expected Result: The report should be submitted successfully, and a confirmation message should appear.

Title: View a Report
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the report list page.
2. Locate the report with the specified incident type and location.
3. Click on the report to view its details.
4. Verify that the report details are displayed correctly.
Expected Result: The report details should be displayed correctly, including the incident type and location.

Title: Receive Notification

PROJECT OVERVIEW:
The application allows users to report incidents, view reports, log in and out, and manage their own reports. It also includes non-functional requirements such as data privacy, reliability, compatibility, performance, and storage space.

TEST CASES

Title: Maintain clean and intuitive graphics throughout the application development
Test Steps:
1. Verify that the application interface remains clean and intuitive during development.
2. Check that all elements are easily accessible and understandable.
Expected Result: The application interface should remain clean and intuitive throughout the development phase.

Title: User can create an account during first launch
Preconditions: The application must be downloaded from the store and installed correctly on the phone.

2. Click on the option to create a new account
3. Fill in the required fields
4. Submit the registration form
Expected Result: The user will be able to navigate within the application after registration.

Preconditions: The user must have registered correctly.
Test Steps:

2. Navigate to the profile settings
3. Update the phone number and residential address
4. Save the changes
Expected Result: The user's personal information related to the application will be updated.
